summary: qontract-reconcile imports httpx typosquat `httpxyz` and forces it into the resolver via uv override
details: Three modules in this package perform top-level `import httpxyz` (reconcile/ldap_users_api/integration.py, reconcile/utils/runtime/integration.py, reconcile/utils/runtime/environment.py), where `httpxyz` is a name-edit typosquat of the popular `httpx` HTTP client. These modules are reachable from the package's CLI entry point, so once `httpxyz` is resolvable in the installer's environment its top-level code executes in the consumer's Python process. pyproject.toml additionally declares `[[tool.uv.dependency-metadata]] name='pagerduty' requires-dist=['httpxyz>=0.31']`, an override that injects `httpxyz` into the resolved dependency graph of any project consuming this source via uv, even though `httpxyz` is not listed as a direct dependency of qontract-reconcile itself. The combination of (a) typosquat-name imports on the CLI's import path and (b) a resolver override that silently pulls the typosquat into downstream installs is a namespace-abuse / dependency-confusion shape: attacker-controlled code (whoever owns `httpxyz` on PyPI) runs in the installer's process whenever the squat resolves.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
